data.title: WTI crude oil rose nearly 4% intraday, while Brent crude oil rose 3.65% intraday.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices continued to rise, with WTI crude oil reaching $92 per barrel, up nearly 4% on the day. Brent crude oil rose 3.65% to $93.68 per barrel.

data.title: Federal Reserve Nominee Kevin Warsh Comments on Inflation Trends
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Kevin Warsh has expressed optimism regarding the current inflation trends. According to NS3.AI, Warsh noted that the trajectory of inflation is showing positive signs, suggesting a favorable economic outlook. His comments come amid ongoing discussions about monetary policy and its impact on the economy.

data.title: ElizaOS Faces Class-Action Lawsuit Over Alleged False Advertising and Market Manipulation
data.description: ElizaOS, formerly known as AI16Z, is currently embroiled in a class-action lawsuit concerning accusations of false advertising and market manipulation. According to NS3.AI, the plaintiffs allege that the developers of ElizaOS misappropriated the branding of Andreessen Horowitz's a16z and falsely marketed their manually operated system as an AI agent with independent investment capabilities. The lawsuit claims that these actions have misled investors and manipulated market perceptions, prompting legal action against the company.

data.title: U.S. Vice President's Trip to Pakistan Remains Uncertain Amid Policy Discussions
data.description:
U.S. Vice President Vance's departure from Washington to Pakistan is currently uncertain, according to BlockBeats. CNN reports that the White House plans to hold further meetings on Tuesday to discuss subsequent matters, with Vice President Vance participating.

A White House official stated, "The White House is holding more policy meetings, and the Vice President will attend these meetings." Vance was originally scheduled to depart for Islamabad on Tuesday morning. However, a crucial issue remains unresolved: whether the Iranian delegation will accompany him.

As of late Tuesday morning local time, Iran has not yet decided whether to send officials to Pakistan for the second round of talks.

data.title: Fed Chair Nominee Wash: Trump Has Not Asked for Pre-Determined Rate Cuts
data.description: Fed Chair nominee Wash stated that U.S. President Donald Trump has never requested him to pre-determine interest rates, and he would not agree to such a request. According to Jin10, Wash emphasized his commitment to maintaining independence in monetary policy decisions during his nomination hearing.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Political Groups Amass $180 Million for Midterm Elections
data.description: Political groups associated with the cryptocurrency industry have accumulated a substantial $180 million fund in preparation for this year's midterm elections. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential for these groups to outspend even some of the Republican Party's most notable super PACs. This financial power underscores the growing influence of the cryptocurrency sector in political arenas, as these groups aim to sway policy and regulatory decisions in their favor. The amassed funds reflect the industry's strategic efforts to secure favorable outcomes in the upcoming elections, potentially impacting legislative directions related to digital currencies.

data.title: Fed Chair Nominee Waller: Inflation Damage Has Improved in Recent Years
data.description: Fed Chair nominee Christopher Waller stated that the inflation damage seen in recent years has shown signs of improvement. According to Jin10, Waller emphasized the progress made in addressing inflationary pressures, which have been a significant concern for the U.S. economy. He noted that while challenges remain, the measures taken have started to yield positive results. Waller's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the Federal Reserve's approach to monetary policy and its impact on inflation. The nominee's perspective highlights the importance of continued efforts to stabilize prices and support economic growth.

data.title: Coinbase's advisory board released a report stating that the threat of quantum computing is looming, and the crypto industry needs to develop countermeasures as soon as possible.
data.description: A 50-page report commissioned by Coinbase argues that while current quantum computers are insufficient to break the encryption technologies of networks like Bitcoin and Ethereum, fault-tolerant large-scale quantum computers will eventually be built, and the crypto industry must begin preparing now. The report, authored by an independent advisory committee including cryptographers and academics such as Dan Boneh of Stanford University, Justin Drake of the Ethereum Foundation, and Sreeram Kannan of Eigen Labs, states that the estimated timeframe for quantum computers to break current encryption standards ranges from several years to over a decade. The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) recommends migrating to quantum-resistant cryptography by 2035, a timeline the report considers potentially optimistic. Quantum-resistant cryptography already exists and is being standardized, but post-quantum digital signatures can be tens to hundreds of times larger than existing signatures, potentially increasing block size by 38 times and posing challenges such as wallet migration. The Ethereum Foundation has proposed quantum-resistant digital signature schemes, and companies like Solana are experimenting with quantum-resistant wallet designs. The report recommends flexible transition strategies, such as hybrid systems, to avoid sacrificing current security while preparing for future upgrades.

data.title: Bank of America Raises Morgan Stanley Price Target Following Strong Q1 Earnings
data.description: Bank of America has increased its price target for Morgan Stanley to $225 from $220, maintaining a buy rating on the stock. According to NS3.AI, this decision comes after Morgan Stanley reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings. The financial institution posted earnings per share (EPS) of $3.43 and revenue of $20.58 billion, surpassing consensus estimates.

data.title: Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh Proposes Overhaul of Central Bank Policies
data.description: Kevin Warsh, the nominee for Federal Reserve Chair, addressed a Senate committee on April 21, outlining his plans for a significant overhaul of the central bank's policymaking framework if confirmed. According to NS3.AI, Warsh emphasized the necessity for new policy tools and communication strategies to enhance the effectiveness of the Federal Reserve. His proposals include modifications to forward guidance, economic projections, and the dot plot, aiming to improve transparency and adaptability in monetary policy.

data.title: Iran Yet to Confirm Delegation for Islamabad Peace Talks
data.description: Pakistan's information minister announced that Iran has not formally confirmed its participation in the upcoming peace talks in Islamabad. According to NS3.AI, this statement suggests that the discussions are pending Iran's official response, leaving the status of the talks uncertain.

data.title: AI16Z Faces Legal Action Over Token Launch and Rebranding
data.description: Burwick Law has initiated a federal class action lawsuit in the Southern District of New York against AI16Z and ELIZAOS creator Walters. The legal action follows the token's market capitalization reaching over $2.6 billion at its peak. According to NS3.AI, the complaint accuses the defendants of consumer protection violations, false advertising, and unjust enrichment related to claims that the project's AI agent could autonomously invest. Legal documents reveal that the token was initially launched as AI16Z on October 24, 2024, and subsequently rebranded as ELIZAOS. The token's value peaked at approximately $2.47 on January 2, 2025, before experiencing a decline due to sell-offs by large holders.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Warsh: Will be independent of US President Trump
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Warsh: Will be independent of US President Trump. Federal Reserve Chair nominee Warsh: I don't know why President Trump chose me. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 76,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.24% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 21, 2026, 14:56 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 76,000 USDT and is now trading at 75,964.960938 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.24% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Mexico Enhances Security at Tourist Sites Following Shooting Incident
data.description: Mexico has announced plans to increase security measures at archaeological and popular tourist locations. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision follows a tragic incident where a Canadian tourist was shot and killed by a gunman, who then took his own life, at the historic Teotihuacan ruins on Monday. The Mexican government aims to ensure the safety of visitors and prevent similar occurrences in the future. Authorities are working on implementing enhanced security protocols to protect both tourists and the country's cultural heritage sites.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Nominee Walsh: Presidents Typically Favor Rate Cuts
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair nominee, Walsh, has stated that U.S. Presidents generally prefer to lower interest rates. According to Jin10, Walsh's comments highlight a common inclination among Presidents to support monetary policies that reduce borrowing costs. This perspective aligns with the broader economic strategy of fostering growth through more accessible credit. Walsh's nomination comes at a time when the Federal Reserve's policy decisions are closely scrutinized, given their significant impact on the economy. The nominee's stance may influence future discussions on interest rate adjustments.

data.title: The creators of AI16Z and ELIZAOS are facing a class-action lawsuit for alleged false advertising and unjust enrichment.
data.description: Crypto law firm Burwick Law has filed a federal class-action lawsuit in the Southern District of New York against Walters, the creator of AI16Z and ELIZAOS, and other defendants, accusing them of violating consumer protection laws, false advertising, and unjust enrichment. Legal documents show that the defendants are accused of using the Andreessen Horowitz brand reputation to package the project and issuing the AI16Z token on Solana on October 24, 2024, later renamed ELIZAOS. The complaint alleges that the project claimed to have an AI agent capable of autonomous investment, but in reality, it was operated manually and generated no revenue during the litigation period. On January 2, 2025, the token price reached an all-time high of approximately $2.47, with a market capitalization exceeding $2.6 billion, before subsequently declining due to large-scale selling. On-chain data shows that the most profitable traders realized approximately $39 million in profits.

data.title: Pakistan's Information Minister: Still awaiting a formal response from Iran regarding participation in peace talks.
data.description: Pakistan's Information Minister: We are still awaiting a formal response from Iran confirming the participation of its delegation in the Islamabad peace talks. (Jinshi)

data.title: Warsh: The Fed needs a completely new inflation framework
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Warsh stated that if appointed and appointed to lead the Fed, he will push for comprehensive policy reforms. Warsh told a Senate committee, "In my view, the Fed needs fundamental policy reform. The missteps in dealing with inflation during the COVID-19 pandemic demand such reforms, and I think that means a shift in how policy is implemented. I think that means a different, entirely new inflation framework." Warsh stated that we need new tools, we need new ways of communicating, mentioning forward guidance, economic projections, and the dot plot. He said the Fed should wait until its meetings to issue statements because projections can hinder decision-making. (Jinshi)

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Indices Experience Decline
data.description: U.S. stock indices faced a downturn as the trading session progressed. According to Jin10, the S&P 500 index saw its gains narrow to less than 0.1%, while both the Dow Jones Industrial Average and the Nasdaq Composite Index reduced their gains to 0.2%. This movement reflects a cautious sentiment among investors amid ongoing market volatility.
